---
title: "Chapter 4.1: Optimal Individualized Treatment Regimes"
format: html
---

*This chapter is a placeholder and will be filled in with content on estimating optimal individualized treatment regimes using the `tlverse` package.*

## Planned content

- Defining optimal treatment regimes as causal estimands
- The relationship between CATEs, blip functions, and optimal rules
- Estimation with the `tmle3mopttx` package from `tlverse`
- Super Learner for the blip function
- Worked example with simulated data
- Diagnostics and interpretation

## Sources and further reading

- van der Laan MJ, Luedtke AR (2015). Targeted learning of the mean outcome under an optimal dynamic treatment rule. *Journal of Causal Inference* 3(1):61-95.
- Luedtke AR, van der Laan MJ (2016). Statistical inference for the mean outcome under a possibly non-unique optimal treatment strategy. *Annals of Statistics* 44(2):713-742.
- `tlverse` R packages: [tlverse.org](https://tlverse.org)
